Car Servicing
Example Customer name: Ian Croft
Cars details Registration number: 1 ………………. Make and model: Vida (4-door saloon) Date of manufacture: 2001 Number of miles 2 ……………. Problems · needs two new 3 ………….. · the inside 4 ………….. needs fixing · one passage seat 5 ………… is stuck · the 6 ……………. needs replacing · the 7 ………… brakes of the car need attention · needs a spare 8 ……………… Service arrangements Date/Time: Tuesday 5 October 8.00 a.m. Courtesy car? 9 ………… Yes, preferably Customer details Address: 361 College Road, Norwich Phone: 10 ……………….

question_groups.prompt_html:
Questions 11–15

Choose the correct letter, A, B or C.

11 Why David Laws leading the meeting instead of Penny Williams today?

A Penny Williams is not well recently

B David Laws is the director now

C The meeting will be postponed

12 What does David say has been a problem with guest speaker talks?

A expensive tickets

B lack of publicity

C inconvenient time

13 What did the man say about break-in at library?

A the building was destroyed to some damage

B some valuable items were stolen

C the thieves had a good preparation

14 Why they want to make an extension of the library?

A it has no renovation since founded

B it need to double the number of computers

C increased number of visiting people

15 The investment from the city council will be deployed for

A extra staff recruitment

B buy more computers

C improve the lighting

items.prompt_html: 11 Why David Laws leading the meeting instead of Penny Williams today?

options.label: A
options.text: Penny Williams is not well recently

options.label: B
options.text: David Laws is the director now

items.prompt_html: 12 What does David say has been a problem with guest speaker talks?
13 What did the man say about break-in at library?

options.label: A
options.text: the building was destroyed to some damage

options.label: C
options.text: the thieves had a good preparation

items.prompt_html: 14 Why they want to make an extension of the library?

options.label: A
options.text: it has no renovation since founded

options.label: B
options.text: it need to double the number of computers

items.prompt_html: 15 The investment from the city council will be deployed for

question_groups.prompt_html:
Questions 21–25

Choose the correct letter, A, B or C.

The artist Samuel Prout

21 According to Jack, why did Samuel Prout never become very famous?

A His output was very limited.

B He has seen as out-fashioned.

C His work never had a major exhibition.

22 Dr Rubin and Jack agree that many of Prout’s problems were caused by

A poor health.

B jealous colleagues.

C an unhappy marriage.

23 How did Prout learn painting?

A His father taught him.

B He went to art galleries.

C He copied pictures.

24 What point does Jack make about Prout’s style?

A Prout used different styles simultaneously.

B Many other artists had a similar style.

C Prout’s style developed throughout his career.

25 What did John Ruskin admire about Prout’s paintings?

A their realism

B their presentation of buildings

C their wide range of subject matters

items.prompt_html: 21 According to Jack, why did Samuel Prout never become very famous?

options.label: C
options.text: His work never had a major exhibition.

items.prompt_html: 22 Dr Rubin and Jack agree that many of Prout’s problems were caused by
23 How did Prout learn painting?
24 What point does Jack make about Prout’s style?

options.label: B
options.text: Many other artists had a similar style.

options.label: C
options.text: Prout’s style developed throughout his career.

items.prompt_html: 25 What did John Ruskin admire about Prout’s paintings?

options.label: C
options.text: their wide range of subject matters

question_groups.instruction:
What information do the speakers give about each of the following pictures?
Choose FIVE correct answers, A–G, next to questions 26–30.
question_groups.prompt_html:
Questions 26–30

What information do the speakers give about each of the following pictures?
Choose FIVE correct answers, A–G, next to questions 26–30.

Information

Pictures

A view in Germany: at Wurzburg 26 …………
Bridge on the Ouse, near York 27 …………….
Wooden scene 28 …………
Venice 29 ……………
Entrance to a harbor 30 ………….

A one of several paintings of the same view

B probably an inaccurate title

C may not be genuine

D was never finished

E typical subject matter for Prout

F skillful painting of light and dark

G shows an event that never took place

items.prompt_html: Choose FIVE corect answers, A-G , next to questions 26-30 .
Bridge on the Ouse, near York 27 …………….
Wooden scene 28 …………
Venice 29 ……………
Choose FIVE corect answers, A-G , next to questions 26-30 .

options.label: A
options.text: one of several paintings of the same view

options.label: F
options.text: skillful painting of light and dark

options.label: G
options.text: shows an event that never took place

question_groups.instruction:
Complete the notes below.
Write ONE WORD ONLY for each answer.
question_groups.prompt_html:
Questions 31–40

Complete the notes below.
Write ONE WORD ONLY for each answer.

Case Study: Chocozine’s difficult decision

Background • Smith and his colleagues noticed the spread of mass-customisation in many US 31 ………….
• They decided to apply the idea to chocolate, and allow clients to decide which ingredients they wanted.
• They called their company’s ‘Chocozine’.

The email order • A European retailer wanted 15,000 chocolate bars to use as a 32 ……….. for customers.
• The deal could improve their sales and provide 33 ………….. .

But there were risks: • They might not have sufficient time for the job.
• If they failed, it could affect their finances and their 34 …………..
• They only had three 35 ……….. , which was not enough.

They attempt to subcontract • At first they couldn’t find a subcontract with the right 36 ……….
• A Harvard professor invited Smith to speak to students.
• Many students said it would be a 37 …….. to do the deal.
• One student had a 38 ………. in the chocolate industry.
• But this subcontract had trouble with the unusual 39 ……… of the chocolate bar.
• Chocozine finally declined the order, but their sales improved anyway.

Comment • Accepting 40 ………… orders has created severe problems for many new businesses.

They attempt to subcontract • At first they couldn’t find a subcontract with the right 36 ……….

• One student had a 38 ………. in the chocolate industry.
• But this subcontract had trouble with the unusual 39 ……… of the chocolate bar.
